title: Known merchant small payment
evidence: Prior CupSafe fixture allowed a small USDt merchant payment when the recipient was verified and amount stayed under cap.
advice: Proceed only if the merchant identity is recognized.

title: Unknown contract approval
evidence: Unknown approval requests are high-risk because token allowance can be used after the signature.
advice: Reject the approval and verify the contract from official project links.

title: Bridge requires manual review
evidence: Bridge operations add destination-chain, route, and finality risk even when the route is known.
advice: Confirm destination chain, recipient address, bridge status, and amount before signing.

title: Spend exceeds bankroll cap
evidence: A verified recipient does not override the daily bankroll cap.
advice: Lower the amount or require a separate manual approval.

title: High slippage swap
evidence: High slippage can indicate thin liquidity, price impact, or a malicious route.
advice: Reduce slippage, split the order, or use a deeper liquidity route.
